title: Reflected XSS Detected via Search Query Parameter

steps.message: Block the detected XSS payload "<script>alert(1)</script>" and common XSS patterns in the WAF, as applications are certainly vulnerable "unless ﬁlters are in place elsewhere that block requests containing XSS exploits within the query itself has speciﬁed."

Query SIEM/log aggregation tools for additional instances of the identified XSS payload "<script>alert(1)</script>" or variations (e.g., "%3e%3cscript%3ealert(document.cookie)%3c/script%3e", "<ScRiPt>alert(document.cookie)</ScRiPt>") across all URL parameters, per the basic approach of "monitoring responses for the appearance of this same string" to identify the full scope.

params.query: index=web_logs (q="<script>alert(1)</script>" OR q="%3e%3cscript%3ealert(document.cookie)%3c/script%3e" OR q="<ScRiPt>alert(document.cookie)</ScRiPt>") OR (message="<script>alert(1)</script>" OR message="%3e%3cscript%3ealert(document.cookie)%3c/script%3e" OR message="<ScRiPt>alert(document.cookie)</ScRiPt>")

steps.message: Isolate the affected server/application instance if direct compromise is suspected, and perform an immediate scan to "perform a comprehensive test of the application to locate as many individual mated and produces minimal false positives" vulnerabilities.

Review and approve temporary application-level mitigation, such as disabling the vulnerable 'q' parameter or implementing stricter input sanitization rules on the '/search' endpoint, recognizing that "Many applications implement rudimentary blacklist-based ﬁ lters" which are a necessary immediate step.

params.details: Disable or apply aggressive sanitization to 'q' parameter on /search endpoint.

steps.message: Create a high-priority ticket for the development team to implement proper output encoding for all user-supplied input that is reflected in HTML responses, as "This behavior of taking user-supplied input and inserting it into the HTML of the server’s response is one of the signatures of reﬂected XSS vulnerabilities," and direct sanitization attempts might "break the application by encoding the HTML."

params.summary: High: Reflected XSS in Search Query Parameter
params.description: A reflected XSS vulnerability was found in the search functionality. User input in the 'q' parameter is directly reflected in the HTML response without proper encoding. Implement robust output encoding for all dynamic content.

steps.message: Notify relevant stakeholders in the incident response channel about the confirmed XSS vulnerability, detailing immediate containment actions and ongoing remediation efforts.

params.message_text: 🚨 Incident Alert: Reflected XSS detected in search functionality (example.com/search). Immediate WAF blocks implemented. Investigation ongoing, dev ticket created. Severity: HIGH.
